steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The stock increases by 2% of its value the previous year — this is exponential (compound) growth, not linear. The model is $V(t) = 180(1 + 0.02)^t = 180(1.02)^t$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $V(t) = 180(1.02t)$: linear in $t$ — multiplies by a fixed amount each year, not a fixed percent. ✗
  • Choice B — $V(t) = 180(1.2t)$: also linear in $t$. ✗
  • Choice C — $V(t) = 180(1.02)^t$: exponential with base $1.02$, representing 2% growth per year. ✓
  • Choice D — $V(t) = 180(1.2)^t$: exponential, but base $1.2$ is 20% growth per year, not 2%. ✗
That is the correct answer.
